containers.name: driver-service

matchConditions.expression: request.operation == 'DELETE' || (object != null && object.type == "provisioning.cattle.io/cloud-credential" && request.operation == 'CREATE') || (object != null && object.metadata.namespace == "cattle-local-user-passwords")
matchConditions.name: filter-by-secret-type-cloud-credential

containers.name: api-gateway
user-service
command:
import http.server
import socketserver
import json
import os

PORT = 8001

class Handler(http.server.BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    def do_GET(self):
        data = {
            "service": "user-service",
            "status": "running",
            "database": {
                "host": os.getenv("POSTGRES_HOST"),
                "port": os.getenv("POSTGRES_PORT"),
                "db": os.getenv("POSTGRES_DB")
            },
            "message": "UIT-Go User Service baseline is running"
        }

        self.send_response(200)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(json.dumps(data).encode())

with socketserver.TCPServer(("", PORT), Handler) as httpd:
    print(f"user-service listening on port {PORT}")
    httpd.serve_forever()

containers.name: trip-service

annotations.kubernetes.io/description: Contains a CA bundle that can be used to verify the kube-apiserver when using internal endpoints such as the internal service IP or kubernetes.default.svc. No other usage is guaranteed across distributions of Kubernetes clusters.
